title: nasa humboldtiana subsp. roseoalba
intro: Nasa humboldtiana subsp. roseoalba is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Loasaceae, native to the Andean region. It is also known as the "Rose Alba Nasa" and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The leaves and stems of Nasa humboldtiana subsp. roseoalba can be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves.
